title: Bump pillow from 12.2.0 to 12.3.0

labels.name: dependencies
labels.description: Pull requests that update a dependency file

labels.name: python:uv
labels.description: Pull requests that update python:uv code

body:
Bumps [pillow](https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow) from 12.2.0 to 12.3.0.
Release notes

Sourced from pillow's releases.

12.3.0

https://pillow.readthedocs.io/en/stable/releasenotes/12.3.0.html

Removals

  • Remove non-image ImageCms modes #9697 [@​radarhere]

Documentation

  • Add release notes for SBOM and performance improvements #9747 [@​radarhere]
  • Add security release notes #9741 [@​radarhere]
  • Add release notes for Python 3.15 beta wheels #9696 [@​radarhere]
  • ImageFont can also be used with ImageText #9597 [@​radarhere]
  • Additional guidelines for security reports #9659 [@​wiredfool]
  • Fixed typo #9636 [@​radarhere]
  • Added CVEs to 12.2.0 release notes #9591 [@​radarhere]
  • Revise development support information in README #9583 [@​aclark4life]
  • Add INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md #9555 [@​aclark4life]
  • Add STRIDE threat model to security docs #9562 [@​aclark4life]
  • Add CVEs to 12.2.0 release notes #9556 [@​radarhere]
  • Update README with revised security policy #9553 [@​radarhere]
  • Update security policy #9552 [@​aclark4life]
  • Update macOS tested Python versions #9534 [@​radarhere]

Dependencies

  • Update dependency harfbuzz to v14.2.1 #9720 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency mypy to v2 #9653 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency cibuildwheel to v4 #9665 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update github-actions #9655 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency libavif to v1.4.2 #9652 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency lcms2 to v2.19.1 #9651 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency check-jsonschema to v0.37.2 #9650 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update google/oss-fuzz digest to d872252 #9614 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency lcms2 to v2.19 #9609 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency libpng to v1.6.58 - autoclosed #9608 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency harfbuzz to v14 #9610 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency mypy to v1.20.2 #9599 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update github-actions #9611 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Update dependency cibuildwheel to v3.4.1 #9607 [@renovate[bot]]
  • Move dependency versions to single JSON and enable Renovate #9559 [@​hugovk]
  • Updated raqm to 0.10.5 #9557 [@​radarhere]
  • Update dependency cibuildwheel to v3.4.0 #9532 [@renovate[bot]]

Testing

  • Remove matrix.os from benchmark #9735 [@​radarhere]
  • Remove references to libavif patch #9734 [@​radarhere]
  • Add benchmark tests #9654 [@​akx]
  • Use reshape() instead of setting NumPy array shape directly #9728 [@​radarhere]

... (truncated)

Commits
  • bb1d8e8 12.3.0 version bump
  • e63fc48 Add release notes for SBOM and performance improvements (#9747)
  • 13b701b Add release notes for #9679
  • 5564ca7 List methods
  • a0920fd Speed up ImageChops operations (#9738)
  • 07e9a6c Speed up Image.filter() (#9736)
  • a94578c Speed up Image.getchannel(), Image.merge(), Image.putalpha() and `Image...
  • 53e02c4 Speed up Image.fill(), Image.linear_gradient() and `Image.radial_gradient...
  • af03747 Speed up Image.resample() (#9739)
  • 5c9ca56 Speed up alpha_composite, matrix, negative, quantize (#9740)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://dependabot-badges.githubapp.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=pillow&package-manager=uv&previous-version=12.2.0&new-version=12.3.0)](https://docs.github.com/en/github/managing-security-vulnerabilities/about-dependabot-security-updates#about-compatibility-scores) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) ---
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot show ignore conditions` will show all of the ignore conditions of the specified dependency - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) You can disable automated security fix PRs for this repo from the [Security Alerts page](https://github.com/canonical/ulwazi/network/alerts).

title: Playwright errors on Ubuntu 26.04
body:
## Description

When testing on Ubuntu 26.04 (in WSL), the tests requiring Playwright errored:

```
Failed to install browsers
Error: ERROR: Playwright does not support chromium on ubuntu26.04-x64
```

The tests that would use a headless browser were also shown as failed:

When the following var is set, Playwright falls back to its build for Ubuntu 24.04 when the same tests are run, and the same tests pass:

`export PLAYWRIGHT_HOST_PLATFORM_OVERRIDE=ubuntu24.04-x64`

I don't believe Playwright is available for 26.04 yet, but it is the current LTS for Ubuntu, so perhaps this should be mentioned in the testing guidance somewhere for contributors.

## Example outputs

### Without setting var

```

E   subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['/home/shane/dev/gh-work/ulwazi/.venv/bin/python3', '-m', 'playwright', 'install', 'chromium']' returned non-zero exit status 1.
------------------------------------------------------------------- Captured stdout call --------------------------------------------------------------------
```

title: refactor: remove sphinx-tabs support
body:
This PR removes support for sphinx-tabs extension completely in line with the sphinx-stack directive under https://documentation.ubuntu.com/sphinx-stack/latest/reference/myst-syntax/#tabs. This maintains the sphinx-design tabs as the default and supported tabs.

I have also decided not to partially support the sphinx-tabs extension due do future maintenance overheads.

## Changes
- Updated the `tabs.py` file to remove the sphinx-tabs logic 
- Updated `conf.py` and python dependancies to remove sphinx-tabs and related settings.
- Committed uv.lock to track the removal
- Updated docs (cheatsheet) to reflect the changes and removed the older guides
- The tabs syntax links now point at the sphinx-stack docs rather than
`sphinx-design.readthedocs.io`, matching the direction of #118:


## Acceptance criteria (as per JIRA ticket)

- [x] sphinx-design is the only recommended and documented tabs option.-
- [x] [x] sphinx-tabs not mentioned in the syntax guides — only the removal notice.
- [x] sphinx-tabs not required, not installed, not built. No workflow referenced
      it directly; dropping the dependency and the test pages takes it out of CI.
- [ ] "sphinx-tabs still working if added back" — deliberately not met. This was
      the optional criterion and it is the one that conflicts with the decision
      above. Re-adding `sphinx-tabs` to `extensions` will render its tabs with
      stock styling, unthemed by Vanilla.

title: docs: Add testing strategy
body:
We are adding a document describing our strategy for testing Ulwazi.
Plus other minor fixes in docs.

title: Version selector is too small and hard to notice
body: Rob Gibbon provided feedback that the version selector on RTD (the default one at the bottom right corner) is very small and too hard to notice.

title: Horizontal menu prototype
body:
Experimenting with search field being in the horizontal menu.
Also removed product menu.
Additionally, removed optional butons from the horizontal menu to make it more minimalistic.

title: Fix spellexception errors after Ulwazi migration
body:
When we apply Ulwazi theme to Charmed Apache Kafka docs set we have these errors about the `spellexception` role.

```text
/home/vladimir.izmalkov@canonical.com/Documents/GitHub/kafka-operator/docs/how-to/client-connections.md.rst:10: WARNING: Unknown interpreted text role "spellexception".
/home/vladimir.izmalkov@canonical.com/Documents/GitHub/kafka-operator/docs/how-to/deploy/deploy-aws.md.rst:10: WARNING: Unknown interpreted text role "spellexception".
```

This seems to be because the spellexception role is no longer supported by Sphinx Stack after changing the spell checker solution.

title: Page duplication in NavMenu
body: When we open the page https://documentation.ubuntu.com/ulwazi/content/test2/ the NavMenu shows the menu item for this page duplicated: This is most likely caused by re-using the same pages twice in TOC. The second time is here - https://github.com/canonical/ulwazi/blob/main/docs/content/test-on-page-toc.rst?plain=1#L14 If it's true, then it's not the theme's bug, but just sample content msitake. We'll need to make sure each page only mentioned once to avoid such effects.

title: Light/dark them switch should change icon
body: Switching between the light and dark themes should be reflected in a change of the button icon. Also, I'd suggest changing the icon from a light bulb to the more often used (and more intuitive?) sun vs moon.

title: Use a more eye-friendly color for dark-mode hover
body: The current hover color for the navig bar is way too contrasting in the dark mode. (And it's also radically different from the color used by the legacy Furo-based theme.) Consider: And (for example):

title: Add Canonical workshop definition for building / running
[Feature request]  Additional help links on RHS bar
body:
Originally, the SP had links to the forums and IRC/matrix in the footers of the page. Later, they got moved to the "more resources" header dropdown, but that's not an intuitive place to find them.

During a discussion with the Ubuntu Marketing counterparts, it was highlighted that we could make the "get help" section much more prominent/easier to find on each page by having these links near the feedback button on the RHS bar, to make the avenues for finding help much more clear.

Specifically, what we're thinking is having links for:
* "Chat to us" (linking to the project's matrix channel)
* "Ask the forums" (linking to the project's discourse)

Somewhere around the "give feedback" button.

WDYT?

title: Avoid use of branding colors in buttons
body: ## Problem Like the old Sphinx theme, Ulwazi currently uses Ubuntu orange in three places: the logo, the search icon, and the feedback button: Using this color as the background of a button does not follow the recommendations in Vanilla: https://vanillaframework.io/docs/patterns/buttons > Brand-colored buttons are deprecated since Vanilla 4.9.0 and will be removed in future version of Vanilla. Please use another type of button instead. My understanding, based on conversations with @juanruitina (designer on the desktop team), is that there are at least two reasons for not using this background color for buttons: 1. It is semantically confusing. Orange buttons are often warnings, and the color itself is close to semantic red (danger). 2. It is an accessibility problem. It is difficult to get good contrast with white text on orange, and it can present readability issues. This is why it is avoided in the UX of Ubuntu Desktop itself. The contrast score for the feedback button is poor: It is improved by making the text black: But is far better with just a white background: Of course, using the orange for the Ubuntu logo is not a problem. That is the brand. The circle of friends does not need to be _read_ in the sense of text. I would also speculate that the occurrence of the other instances of the Ubuntu orange dilutes that branding, scattering it across the page in random places: ## Proposal I would suggest removing the orange in both cases. With the feedback button being replaced with Vanilla's recommendation for a default button or positive action. Default (my preference, I think it looks clean): Positive (also better): ## Examples from canonical.com

title: Main navigation menu takes up too much space on smaller screens
body: (Using Ulwazi 0.5 on Ubuntu) When windows are tiled side-by-side on my screen (width ~950px), a hamburger menu shows up at the top left of the screen. But clicking on it creates an overlay that shows a search bar but not in an aesthetically pleasing way. Also the main navigation menu on the left cannot be hidden and sits in full before the actual page content which takes up unnecessary space.

title: "Toggle side navigation" button does not do anything
body: (Ulwazi 0.5) The "Toggle side navigation" button does not seem to do anything. When the windows are tiled side-by-side on my screen (width ~950px), I assumed the on-page navigation on the right would be hidden and the "Toggle side navigation" button would hide / display it. The whole on-page navigation is displayed currently and takes up a lot of space before the actual page content. I think the behaviour we have in the existing Sphinx Stack theme has a cleaner layout.

title: Gap missing between code block and admoniiton
body: When including an admonition after a code block, a space is missing. This happens on page [how-to/slice-a-package.md](https://github.com/asanvaq/chisel-docs/blob/ulwazi/docs/how-to/slice-a-package.md) in the Chisel documentation under the ulwazi branch. To reproduce, clone the chisel-docs repository, check out the ulwazi branch, build the docs, and navigate to "How-to guides" > "Slice a package". ### Screenshot Rendered page with Ulwazi theme: ### Markdown file Below is the corresponding code block and admonition: ```yaml slices: bins: essential: - libacl1_libs - libc6_libs - libselinux1_libs - libtinfo6_libs - vim-common_addons - vim-common_config - vim-tiny_config contents: /usr/bin/vim.tiny: ``` ```{tip} Sometimes, a package (and thus its slices) may have "essential" dependencies that are not explicitly listed in the package's [`control` file]. E.g. OpenSSL's `c_rehash` requires Perl to run, but the OpenSSL package does not mention it. The same happens frequently for Bash scripts that may assume certain packages (like `coreutils`) are installed. ```

title: Make tracking/analytics configurable

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
Let's make the container ID configurable for Google Tag Manager.
It may be useful later or just for usage outside of Canonical.

title: Additional line is rendered when including code in admonition
body: When including a line of code in an admonition, an additional line is rendered. This happens on page [how-to/slice-a-package.md](https://github.com/asanvaq/chisel-docs/blob/ulwazi/docs/how-to/slice-a-package.md) in the Chisel documentation under the ulwazi branch. To reproduce, clone the chisel-docs repository, check out the ulwazi branch, build the docs, and navigate to "How-to guides" > "Slice a package". ### Screenshot Rendered page with Ulwazi theme: ### Markdown file Below is the corresponding admonition: ```{important} If any of your slices' paths are architecture-specific, you must add the `arch` field. For [example](https://github.com/canonical/chisel-releases/blob/e5db53e97e03c46944a84e7ab5abeb1b7cb53cd9/slices/libc6.yaml#L29): > `/usr/lib/*-linux-*/libmvec.so.*: {arch: [amd64, arm64]}` ```

title: Split Ulwazi extensions into core and optional

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
Core extensions are needed for it to work with minimal docs (like a single empty page or something like that).
Optional extensions are needed for our Ulwazi demo to work.

P.S. We are aiming to include all main fatures in our demo docs to test them.

title: feat: add visual distinction to content that belongs to a tab
body: Currently there is no clear delimiter to indicate the boundary between content that belongs to a tab versus the rest of the page. For example, in the screenshot below (taken from the rendered version of [this page](https://github.com/canonical/mongo-single-kernel-library/blob/ulwazi-test/docs/how-to/deploy/via-juju-cli.md)), the content that belongs to the active tab is just the `juju deploy ...` command. Since there can be irregular and subtle differences between instructions that belong to different tabs, it could be helpful for the user to have a visual indicator of tab boundaries.

title: note block inside of a tab-item cuts off at the margin
body: The full width of the "Note" block inside the "K8s" tab cuts off at the right margin, and there is no way of scrolling to reveal the rest of the content. This happens on page [`how-to/monitoring/view-metrics.md`](https://github.com/canonical/mongo-single-kernel-library/blob/ulwazi-test/docs/how-to/monitoring/view-metrics.md) in the Charmed MongoDB documentation under the `ulwazi-test` branch. To reproduce, clone the `mongo-single-kernel-library` repository, check out the `ulwazi-test` branch, build the docs, and navigate to "How-to guides" > "Monitoring" > "View metrics". ### Screenshots Rendered page with Ulwazi theme: It seems like all contents of {tab-item} overflow. For comparison, this is the [same page](https://canonical-charmed-mongodb.readthedocs-hosted.com/8/how-to/monitoring/view-metrics/) with the current **Furo** theme: ### Markdown file Below is the corresponding section of `view-metrics.md` that is shown in the screenshot. See [`how-to/monitoring/view-metrics.md`](https://github.com/canonical/mongo-single-kernel-library/blob/ulwazi-test/docs/how-to/monitoring/view-metrics.md) for more. `````` (access-metrics-with-grafana)= ## Access metrics with Grafana Charmed MongoDB supports integration with the [Canonical Observability Stack (COS)](https://charmhub.io/topics/canonical-observability-stack), which enables the usage of observability tools like Grafana, Loki, and Prometheus. `````{tab-set} ````{tab-item} VM :sync: vm ````{tab-item} K8s :sync: k8s First, deploy the [`cos-lite` bundle](https://charmhub.io/cos-lite). Follow the [COS deployment tutorial](https://charmhub.io/topics/canonical-observability-stack/tutorials/install-microk8s) Wait for the status of the `cos-lite` bundle to become `active` and `idle`. ```{note} Make sure to use the following offers: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/canonical/cos-lite-bundle/09715997467e1b568109064fa17ecefd4b574032/overlays/offers-overlay.yaml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/canonical/cos-lite-bundle/09715997467e1b568109064fa17ecefd4b574032/overlays/storage-small-overlay.yaml They contain the `prometheus-scrape` endpoint, which is essential to our stack, but was removed from the example offer of the bundle. ``` ``````

title: Test the theme on mobile devices

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body: We need to make sure, that the theme works well on mobile devices by testing it on some of the most relevant environments, including smaller screeens, mobile web-browsers, etc.

title: Tested docs: several build failures
body:
Cross posted in the Starter Pack docs because some of them are documentation issues
https://github.com/canonical/sphinx-docs-starter-pack/issues/580
https://github.com/canonical/sphinx-docs-starter-pack/issues/581

I tried to follow the docs and ran into multiple build failures. Mostly missing deps, but also some missing commas in the code blocks. I haven't managed to get the build to pass yet on my docs.

title: Formatting for grid
body: The formatting and spacing for `grid` and `grid-item-cards` needs to be adjusted.

title: Font size of numbers in numerated lists seems too big
Support ability to apply external-link icon to links

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body:
Sometimes it's useful to have a link open in a new tab. For such links, a common pattern on the web is to put a box-with-arrow icon after the link. Vanilla has a suitable icon called `external-link`. It would be great if Ulwazi supported a way to decorate links with this icon (on a per-link basis).

This issue is only about applying the icon, not about deciding/controlling which links open in a new tab. I think that can be left to the authors of the docs.

---

Additional context, for interest: The [`sphinx-new-tab-link`](https://github.com/ftnext/sphinx-new-tab-link) extension can automatically make external links open in a new tab and apply a similar icon to Vanilla's `external-link` icon. Personally, I don't want to use this extension in my docs, because I don't think external links should be new-tab by default. See [GOV.UK Design System](https://design-system.service.gov.uk/styles/links/#opening-links-in-a-new-tab).

title: `convert_tabs` is too long for a single function
body:
The length and cyclomatic complexity of `convert_tabs` greatly exceeds recommended standards.

This was ignored in my linting of Ulwazi because of its nontrivial nature, but it should be addressed in a follow-up pass.

```bash
PLR0912 Too many branches (55 > 12)
 --> ulwazi/tabs.py:7:5
  |
7 | def convert_tabs(body_html: str) -> str:
  |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^
8 |     """Convert sphinx-tabs markup to vanilla theme classes and structure."""
9 |     if not body_html:
  |

PLR0915 Too many statements (165 > 50)
 --> ulwazi/tabs.py:7:5
  |
7 | def convert_tabs(body_html: str) -> str:
  |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^
8 |     """Convert sphinx-tabs markup to vanilla theme classes and structure."""
9 |     if not body_html:
  |
```

To close this issue, we'll need to:
* Remove the `noqa`
* Split the function into logical pieces and simplify logic when possible

title: Weirdly looking code blocks captions
body: Captions for code blocks look a bit off. Let's think how we can improve them. Ideally, discuss and get an approval fromd esigners and other TAs before merging.

title: Sudoptimal tables design

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body: The current table design is based on Vanilla Framework and lacks many lines in tables. It may look OK for product websites with smaller datasets, but it doesn't look right for bigger tables with a lot of data. I think we need to investigate the alternatives, discuss them with the Design team, get an approval for a better tables design, and implement it for the Ulwazi.

title: Improve table columns width auto-adjustment

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body: The old Furo-based theme has great table formatting with auto-adjustment of column width depending on content. Our Ulwazi tables seem to be lacking this feature. Because of that, some tables look much worse. Let's see what we can do. Maybe we can bring some of it to the Ulwazi.
